title: Line Counter
shortDescription: Count the number of lines in pasted text, including blank lines when they are present.
metaDescription: Count lines in pasted text, including blank lines, and review the total in a clear, simple format.
intro: Paste your text and get the line count immediately, including empty lines if they are part of the input.
overview:
This line counter gives you the total number of lines in any text you paste into the box. It is useful when you need to verify a draft, compare versions of a file, or check whether copied content includes empty lines that matter.

The count is based on line breaks in the text you provide. If your content ends with a trailing newline, that can affect the total, so it is worth checking the result against the exact text you pasted. For long notes, code snippets, and structured text, the tool helps you see the line structure without manual counting.
howItWorks: Paste or type your text into the input area.
The tool counts the lines from the exact text, including blank lines.
Read the total and compare it with your source if you need to confirm formatting.

useCases.title: Reviewing a draft before sending
useCases.description: Check how many lines your email, report, or note contains before you share it.

useCases.title: Checking pasted code blocks
useCases.description: Count the lines in code snippets to confirm structure after copying from an editor or chat app.

useCases.title: Comparing two versions of text
useCases.description: See whether an edited paragraph or document changed in length after line breaks were added or removed.

useCases.title: Verifying structured content
useCases.description: Use it for lists, outlines, lyrics, or transcripts where the number of lines affects layout or reading flow.

examples.title: Short note
examples.input:
First line
Second line
Third line
examples.description: Three visible lines produce a total of 3.

examples.title: Text with a blank line
examples.description: The empty middle line is counted as a line because it is part of the input.

examples.title: Trailing newline
examples.description: A final line break can add an extra counted line, so the ending matters.

faq.question: Does a blank line count as a line?
faq.answer: Yes. If an empty line exists between two line breaks, it is counted as a line because it is part of the text structure.

faq.question: Why did the total increase after I added a line break at the end?
faq.answer: A trailing newline can create an additional line boundary. If your source ends with a final return, the count may be higher than the visible text suggests.

faq.question: How should I interpret pasted text from editors or chat apps?
faq.answer: The result reflects the exact characters you paste, so copied formatting, hidden line breaks, and extra returns all affect the total.

faq.question: What is the most common counting mistake?
faq.answer: People often count only visible text and forget blank lines or the final newline at the end of the content.

seo.title: Line Counter Tool
seo.description: Count lines in pasted text, including blank lines, and review the total in a clear, simple format.
